clauses.text: Students are required to maintain a minimum attendance of 75% in each course to be eligible for examinations. Attendance is recorded for all scheduled classes, including lectures, tutorials, and practical sessions.

Medical absences must be supported by a valid medical certificate issued by a registered medical practitioner. Such certificates must be submitted to the course coordinator within three working days of the absence.
Students with attendance below 75% may be denied permission to sit for the final examinations. In such cases, they will be required to repeat the course in the subsequent semester.
Attendance records are maintained electronically and are accessible to students through the university portal. Any discrepancies must be reported to the department head within one week of the class.

Participation in university-approved extracurricular activities, such as sports events or cultural programs, may be considered as valid attendance upon submission of official documentation from the organizing body.

To be eligible for semester examinations, students must have completed all assigned coursework, including assignments, projects, and laboratory work, with satisfactory performance as determined by the course instructor.

Students must clear all outstanding dues, including tuition fees and library fines, before being allowed to register for examinations. The examination office will verify this during the registration process.

Eligibility for practical examinations requires completion of all laboratory sessions and submission of lab reports. Students failing to meet these requirements will be barred from the practical component.
In cases of academic misconduct, such as plagiarism or cheating, students may be disqualified from the examination and subject to disciplinary action as per the university's code of conduct.
International students must comply with visa regulations and provide necessary documentation to confirm their eligibility to continue studies and sit for examinations.
Students on academic probation are required to meet additional criteria, including attendance in counseling sessions, to regain eligibility for examinations.

Students may apply for re-evaluation of their answer scripts within 15 days of the result declaration. Applications must be submitted online through the university portal with a fee of INR 500 per paper.
Re-evaluation is limited to checking for totaling errors, un-evaluated portions, or improper assessment. It does not include re-grading of answers or reconsideration of marking schemes.
If a discrepancy is found during re-evaluation, the marks will be updated accordingly, and the fee will be refunded. No changes will be made if the original assessment is deemed correct.
Re-evaluation results will be communicated to students via email and the university portal within 30 days of application submission. No further appeals are permitted beyond this process.
Students who have passed the examination are not eligible for re-evaluation unless they suspect a computational error that could affect their grade classification.
Merit-based scholarships are awarded to students securing a CGPA of 8.5 or above in the previous semester. Renewal is contingent upon maintaining this performance in subsequent semesters.

Need-based scholarships require submission of income certificates from competent authorities. The scholarship amount is determined based on family income and is disbursed in two installments per year.

Sports scholarships are available for students excelling in inter-university competitions. Recipients must participate in at least two events annually and maintain academic performance above 7.0 CGPA.
Scholarship recipients are required to maintain good conduct and adhere to university rules. Any disciplinary action may result in suspension or termination of the scholarship.

Applications for scholarships must be submitted by the end of the first week of each semester. Late applications will not be considered, and scholarships are awarded on a first-come, first-served basis subject to availability.
International students may apply for scholarships funded by external agencies, provided they meet the eligibility criteria set by the funding body and comply with university admission requirements.
All undergraduate students in professional courses must complete a mandatory internship of at least 8 weeks during their summer vacation after the fourth semester.

Internships must be undertaken with organizations approved by the university's placement cell. Students are required to submit a proposal outlining the internship objectives and company details for approval.
Upon completion, students must submit an internship report, performance certificate from the employer, and a presentation to the department faculty for evaluation.

Failure to complete the internship requirement will result in withholding of the degree certificate until the requirement is fulfilled. Extensions may be granted for valid reasons with prior approval.
Students are encouraged to seek internships aligned with their career goals. The placement cell provides guidance and organizes industry interactions to facilitate internship opportunities.
Hostel accommodation is allocated based on merit, distance from residence, and availability. Priority is given to first-year students and those from outside the city.
Residents must adhere to a curfew of 10:00 PM on weekdays and 11:00 PM on weekends. Late arrivals require prior permission from the warden and may incur penalties.
Visitors are allowed only during designated hours (10:00 AM to 6:00 PM) and must be registered at the reception. Overnight stays by visitors are strictly prohibited.
Maintenance of cleanliness and hygiene is mandatory. Residents are responsible for their rooms and common areas. Violations may lead to fines or eviction.
Consumption of alcohol, tobacco, or any prohibited substances is strictly forbidden within hostel premises. Offenders will face disciplinary action, including expulsion.
Hostel fees must be paid by the 5th of each month. Late payments will result in disconnection of utilities and may lead to termination of accommodation.
